question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the DEMO network completion path in Downloader.downloadNextBatch() to force setEnabled(false), halting all future Hashgraph history ingestion and causing a permanent gap in the mirror node's record of consensus history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is called when dataFilename.getInstant().isAfter(endDate) to craft a stream file with a future timestamp, causing the downloader to permanently disable itself and freeze the Hashgraph history at a manipulated point?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the consensusRatio field exposed via getCommon() being set to its minimum (0) to allow a single malicious node's signature to satisfy consensus, enabling injection of tampered Hashgraph history files that pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the downloadRatio field in CommonDownloaderProperties (accessible via getCommon()) being set below consensusRatio to cause the downloader to query fewer nodes than required for consensus, allowing a minority of colluding nodes to serve tampered stream files that get accepted as valid Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endpointOverride field in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) to redirect stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server that serves forged Hashgraph history files, bypassing the legitimate cloud storage source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the allowAnonymousAccess flag in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being enabled to access and enumerate stream files from the cloud bucket without credentials, facilitating reconnaissance for a targeted Hashgraph history tampering attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the bucketName field in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being misconfigured or overridable to point the downloader at an attacker-controlled bucket containing fabricated Hashgraph history stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathPrefix field in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) to inject path traversal sequences that redirect stream file resolution to attacker-controlled paths, causing the downloader to ingest tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxSize field in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to its minimum (2 bytes) to cause all legitimate stream files to be rejected as oversized, creating a denial-of-history condition where no Hashgraph records are ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the frequency Duration returned by getFrequency() being set to an extremely large value to introduce massive delays in Hashgraph history ingestion, creating exploitable windows where the mirror node's view of history is stale and manipulable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType returned by getStreamType() to cause the downloader to process BALANCE stream files as RECORD stream files (or vice versa), corrupting the Hashgraph history by associating incorrect data with consensus timestamps?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isPersistBytes() returning false (default) to ensure that raw stream file bytes are discarded after processing, preventing forensic verification of tampered Hashgraph history files that were ingested before detection?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteFiles() returning false (default) to ensure that no local archive copies of stream files are written, eliminating the audit trail needed to detect and prove Hashgraph history tampering after the fact?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteSignatures() returning false (default) to ensure that signature files are never archived locally, destroying the cryptographic evidence needed to verify the integrity of ingested Hashgraph history and detect retroactive tampering?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batchSize field in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to 1 to force the downloader to process stream files one at a time, enabling a timing attack where tampered Hashgraph history files are injected between legitimate batches?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timeout Duration in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to an extremely short value to cause legitimate node signature downloads to time out, reducing the effective node count below the consensusRatio threshold and allowing a smaller set of potentially compromised nodes to dictate accepted Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathType field (ACCOUNT_ID, AUTO, NODE_ID) in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to AUTO to cause ambiguous path resolution that selects attacker-controlled node paths over legitimate ones, leading to ingestion of tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pathRefreshInterval in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being set to a very large value to prevent the downloader from refreshing node paths, causing it to continue using stale or attacker-substituted paths that serve tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shouldDownload() check that calls isEnabled() to race-condition the enabled flag between the check and the actual download operation, causing the downloader to process a tampered stream file that was injected during the race window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sources list in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being empty or misconfigured to cause the downloader to fall back to an attacker-controlled source that serves fabricated Hashgraph history stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SourceType.LOCAL cloud provider option in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) to redirect stream file downloads to a local filesystem path that the attacker has write access to, enabling injection of tampered Hashgraph history files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the accessKey and secretKey fields in CommonDownloaderProperties (via getCommon()) being logged or exposed through metrics/actuator endpoints to gain cloud storage credentials, then use those credentials to replace legitimate stream files with tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio defaulting to exactly 1/3 (BigDecimal.ONE.divide(BigDecimal.valueOf(3))) to craft a scenario where exactly 1/3 of nodes serve tampered stream files, meeting the minimum threshold and causing the tampered Hashgraph history to be accepted as consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled() has no access control or validation to toggle the downloader off via an exposed configuration endpoint (e.g., Spring Boot Actuator), creating gaps in Hashgraph history ingestion that allow tampered history to be inserted when re-enabled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setPersistBytes(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit setPersistBytes() being callable without authentication to enable byte persistence, then trigger a large volume of oversized stream file downloads that exhaust disk space and cause the importer to crash, creating a gap in Hashgraph history?
